en.extract:
The black lory (Chalcopsitta atra),  is a medium-sized, blackish parrot with black bill, dark grey feet and long rounded tail. It has yellow and red under-tail. The sexes are similar.  It is native to West Papua in Indonesia.
The black lory is evaluated as Least Concern on IUCN Red List of Threatened Species.


Description
The black lory is 32 cm (13 in) long and has a black bill. Its plumage is mostly black with a blue rump. Red markings on face, thighs, and tail vary between the three subspecies. Males and females are similar in external appearance.


Taxonomy
The species was given its formal name by the naturalist Giovanni Antonio Scopoli. Scopoli did not in fact examine any specimens, but used the informal description by the explorer Pierre Sonnerat published in his book Voyage à la Nouvelle-Guinée (1776). The determination was accurate enough for the name to be valid, so Scopoli is credited as the author of this taxon.
Three subspecies of the black lory are recognized:
Chalcopsitta atra (Scopoli, 1786)

Chalcopsitta atra atra (Scopoli, 1786), native to western Bird's Head Peninsula of the Indonesian province of West Papua, and nearby islands.
Chalcopsitta atra bernsteini Rosenberg, 1861, on the Indonesian island of Misool.
Chalcopsitta atra insignis Oustalet, 1878, on eastern Bird's Head Peninsula, nearby islands, and the Onin and Bomberai Peninsulas of West Papua.


References


Cited texts
Forshaw, Joseph M. (2006). Parrots of the World; an Identification Guide. Illustrated by Frank Knight. Princeton University Press. ISBN 0-691-09251-6.


External links

BirdLife Species Factsheet

The '''black lory''' ('''''Chalcopsitta atra'''''),  is a medium-sized, blackish [[parrot]] with black [[beak|bill]], dark grey feet and long rounded tail. It has yellow and red under-tail. The sexes are similar.  It is native to West Papua in Indonesia.

The black [[Lories and lorikeets|lory]] is evaluated as Least Concern on [[IUCN Red List]] of Threatened Species.<ref name="iucn status" />

==Description==
The black lory is {{convert|32|cm|in|abbr=on}} long and has a black bill. Its plumage is mostly black with a blue rump. Red markings on face, thighs, and tail vary between the three subspecies. Males and females are similar in external appearance.<ref name="Forshaw (2006). plate 7.">Forshaw (2006). plate 7.</ref>

==Taxonomy==
The species was given its [[Species name|formal name]] by the naturalist [[Giovanni Antonio Scopoli]]. Scopoli did not in fact examine any specimens, but used the informal description by the explorer [[Pierre Sonnerat]] published in his book ''Voyage à la Nouvelle-Guinée'' (1776). The determination was accurate enough for the name to be valid, so Scopoli is credited as the [[Author citation (zoology)|author of this taxon]].<ref>{{cite book |url=https://archive.org/details/scopolisornithol00scop |title=Scopoli's Ornithological papers from his Deliciae florae et faunae insubricae (Ticini: 1786-1788) |last=Newton |first=Alfred |publisher=The Willughby Society |year=1882 |location=London}}</ref>

Three subspecies of the black lory are recognized:<ref name = zoonomen>{{cite web | url =http://www.zoonomen.net/avtax/psit.html |title = Zoological Nomenclature Resource: Psittaciformes (Version 9.022) |date =2009-03-28|publisher = www.zoonomen.net }}</ref>

''Chalcopsitta atra'' <small>(Scopoli, 1786)</small>
* ''Chalcopsitta atra atra'' <small>(Scopoli, 1786)</small>, native to western [[Bird's Head Peninsula]] of the Indonesian province of [[West Papua (province)|West Papua]], and nearby islands.<ref name="Forshaw (2006). plate 7."/>
* ''Chalcopsitta atra bernsteini'' <small>Rosenberg, 1861</small>, on the Indonesian island of [[Misool]].<ref name="Forshaw (2006). plate 7."/>
* ''Chalcopsitta atra insignis'' <small>Oustalet, 1878</small>, on eastern Bird's Head Peninsula, nearby islands, and the Onin and [[Bomberai Peninsula]]s of West Papua.<ref name="Forshaw (2006). plate 7."/>

zh.extract:
黑鹦鹉（學名：Chalcopsitta atra，英文：Black Lory，台湾作黑色吸蜜鸚鵡），暱稱黑猩猩，分布在印度尼西亚和新几内亚部分地区。身长在30厘米左右，栖息在各种低地地区。具有较强攻击性，喜爱洗澡。
公鸟与母鸟轮流孵蛋，繁殖期大约在4-12月，一次产卵2颗，孵化期约25天，羽化期三个月。
